title: Complex<Q>: Type and Value
analysis: Task adds complex number support to the Reify type system at the foundational reify-types level. The Type enum (ty.rs) currently has 18 variants with #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]; adding Complex(Box<Type>) follows the established compound-type pattern used by List, Set, Option, Point, Vector, Tensor. The Value enum (value.rs) currently has 14 variants with manual PartialEq, Ord, Display, and content_hash implementations; each must gain a Complex arm. Content hash tags 0-14 are allocated (10 reserved for Satisfaction), so Complex gets tag 15. Ord type_tag values 0-13 are allocated, so Complex gets tag 14. The f64 comparison pattern (to_bits for equality, NaN canonicalization for hashing) is well-established via Real and Scalar and will be applied to both re and im components. The dimension field follows Scalar's pattern — both re and im share one DimensionVector (e.g., complex impedance in ohms). Factory method Type::complex(q) follows the vec2/vec3/point2/point3/tensor pattern. All changes are confined to crates/reify-types; downstream crates with exhaustive matches on Type/Value will need separate wildcard or explicit arms (out of scope for this task but noted: reify-lsp/analysis.rs, reify-constraints/classifier.rs, reify-expr/lib.rs, reify-stdlib/lib.rs, reify-eval/lib.rs, reify-compiler/lib.rs, reify-constraints/cpsat.rs, reify-constraints/solver.rs).

steps.description: Write failing tests in ty.rs::tests for Type::Complex: (a) Display format — Complex<Real>, Complex<Scalar[m]>, nested Complex<Complex<Real>>; (b) factory method Type::complex(q) == Type::Complex(Box::new(q)); (c) Eq/Hash consistency via HashMap insert+lookup for complex(Real) vs complex(Int); (d) is_numeric() returns false; (e) as_name() returns None.

Add Complex(Box<Type>) variant to Type enum in ty.rs. Add Display arm: Complex(inner) => write!(f, "Complex<{}>", inner). Add factory: pub fn complex(q: Type) -> Self { Type::Complex(Box::new(q)) }. No changes needed for is_numeric or as_name (existing catch-alls and non-matching patterns handle them correctly).

Write failing tests in value.rs::tests for Value::Complex Display: (a) dimensionless positive imaginary — Complex { re: 3.0, im: 4.0, dimension: DIMENSIONLESS } displays as '3+4i'; (b) negative imaginary — re: 3.0, im: -4.0 displays as '3-4i'; (c) fractional parts — re: 3.5, im: 4.2 displays as '3.5+4.2i'; (d) dimensioned with LENGTH — (3+4i) m; (e) zero imaginary — 3+0i; (f) negative real — -3-4i.

Write failing tests in value.rs::tests for Value::Complex PartialEq: (a) equal values with same re/im/dimension; (b) different re not equal; (c) different im not equal; (d) different dimension not equal; (e) -0.0 vs 0.0 distinguished via to_bits; (f) Complex != Real with same numeric value; (g) Complex != Scalar with same value and dimension.

Write failing tests in value.rs::tests for Value::Complex Ord: (a) Complex sorts after Tensor (type_tag 14 > 13); (b) Complex sorts before Undef conceptually — verify relative to known-order types; (c) within-type: dimension compared first; (d) same dimension: re bits compared second; (e) same dimension+re: im bits compared third; (f) PartialOrd consistent with Ord.

Write failing tests in value.rs::tests for Value::Complex content_hash: (a) determinism — same value produces same hash across two calls; (b) NaN re canonicalized — two NaN-re values produce same hash; (c) hash-equality consistency — equal values produce equal hashes; (d) different re produces different hash; (e) different dimension produces different hash; (f) Complex(3.0, 0.0, DIMENSIONLESS) hash differs from Scalar(3.0, DIMENSIONLESS) hash (tag 15 vs 4).
Write failing test in value.rs::tests for Value::dimension(): Complex { re: 1.0, im: 2.0, dimension: LENGTH }.dimension() returns LENGTH, not DIMENSIONLESS.

Add Complex { re: f64, im: f64, dimension: DimensionVector } variant to Value enum in value.rs. Add arms in all five trait/method implementations: (1) Display — format each component with Real's whole-number convention, join with +/- sign, append 'i', wrap in parens with dimension suffix if non-dimensionless; (2) PartialEq — compare re.to_bits(), im.to_bits(), and dimension; (3) Ord — type_tag=14 in type_tag(), within-type compare dimension.cmp then re.to_bits().cmp then im.to_bits().cmp; (4) content_hash — tag byte 15, 17-byte buffer [tag, re_bits(8), im_bits(8)] with NaN canonicalization, combine with dimension.content_hash(); (5) dimension() — match Complex { dimension, .. } => *dimension.

design_decisions.decision: Complex is NOT numeric (is_numeric returns false)
design_decisions.rationale: is_numeric() gates single-f64 scalar arithmetic paths. Complex needs distinct two-component arithmetic and cannot be losslessly converted via as_f64(). Keeping it non-numeric prevents silent misuse in scalar contexts and avoids breaking existing numeric dispatch.
Sequential allocation after existing tags. Content hash tags 0-14 are used (10 reserved for Satisfaction). Ord type_tags 0-13 are used (Tensor=13). Complex sorts after Tensor in cross-type ordering.

design_decisions.decision: Display format: re±im·i with Real-style whole-number formatting, parenthesized with dimension if non-dimensionless
design_decisions.rationale: Follows Real's convention (whole numbers without trailing .0, e.g. 3 not 3.0). Negative imaginary uses minus sign directly (3-4i not 3+-4i). Dimensioned values use parentheses — (3+4i) m — to avoid ambiguity between 3+4i·m and (3+4i)·m. Dimensionless values omit dimension entirely for clean output.

design_decisions.decision: Both re and im share one DimensionVector
design_decisions.rationale: Follows physics convention: complex impedance is Z = R + jX where both R and X are in ohms. The dimension applies to the complex number as a whole, not separately to real and imaginary parts. This matches the task specification.

design_decisions.decision: dimension() method gets explicit arm for Complex rather than relying on catch-all
design_decisions.rationale: The existing catch-all returns DIMENSIONLESS, which would silently lose dimension information for Complex values. An explicit arm ensures dimensioned Complex values correctly report their dimension.

design_decisions.decision: f64 comparison uses to_bits() for both re and im, with NaN canonicalization in content_hash
design_decisions.rationale: Mirrors the established Scalar pattern exactly. Preserves -0.0 vs 0.0 distinction for cache coherency. NaN values are equal to themselves via to_bits(), satisfying Rust's Hash contract (a == b implies hash(a) == hash(b)). NaN canonicalization in content_hash prevents different NaN bit patterns from producing different hashes.

reuse.where: ty.rs — List(Box<Type>), Set(Box<Type>), Option(Box<Type>), Point/Vector/Tensor all use Box<Type>
reuse.how: Complex(Box<Type>) follows the identical pattern for recursive type nesting with the inner type as the quantity parameter

reuse.where: ty.rs impl Type — vec2(q), vec3(q), point2(q), point3(q), tensor(r,n,q) all wrap q in Box::new
reuse.how: Type::complex(q) wraps q in Box::new identically: pub fn complex(q: Type) -> Self { Type::Complex(Box::new(q)) }

reuse.what: f64 to_bits() equality and NaN canonicalization patterns
reuse.where: value.rs — PartialEq for Real uses a.to_bits() == b.to_bits(); Scalar uses same for si_value + dimension check; content_hash canonicalizes NaN via if r.is_nan() { f64::NAN.to_bits() } else { r.to_bits() }
reuse.how: Apply identical to_bits() comparison for both re and im in PartialEq; use identical NaN canonicalization for both re and im in content_hash

reuse.where: value.rs — Scalar content_hash builds [tag=4, si_value_bits(8)] then combines with dimension.content_hash()
reuse.how: Complex builds [tag=15, re_bits(8), im_bits(8)] then combines with dimension.content_hash() — same pattern extended to two f64 fields

reuse.where: value.rs — type_tag() function maps each variant to u8, cross-type ordering by tag, within-type match for same-tag comparison
reuse.how: Add type_tag 14 for Complex; within-type comparison mirrors Scalar: dimension.cmp().then_with(|| re_bits.cmp()).then_with(|| im_bits.cmp())

reuse.what: DimensionVector type, constants, and is_dimensionless() method
reuse.where: dimension.rs — DIMENSIONLESS, LENGTH, ANGLE constants; is_dimensionless() check; content_hash() method
reuse.how: Used for Complex's dimension field storage, Display conditional formatting, and content_hash composition
